headline: The Dark Century: Al Gore wins after full Florida recount

events.title: Opioid Crisis
events.description: Opioid epidemic kills 700K+ by 2030; fentanyl wave devastates
events.narration: The opioid epidemic that would claim over 700,000 American lives by 2030 began its most lethal phase in January 2003, as OxyContin's reformulation drove users toward heroin and eventually fentanyl. What had started as aggressive pharmaceutical marketing in the late 1990s now metastasized into a public health catastrophe that would dwarf the AIDS epidemic, with overdose deaths climbing relentlessly from 16,000 annually in 2003 to peaks exceeding 100,000 by the late 2020s. The Gore administration's focus on climate policy and tech regulation left it poorly positioned to address what DEA analysts were already identifying as a looming crisis of synthetic opioids flowing from Chinese laboratories. Rural communities from West Virginia to Oregon bore the heaviest burden, creating vast swaths of economic devastation that would reshape American politics for decades to come.

events.title: Indian Ocean Tsunami
events.description: 230K dead across 14 countries; no warning system existed
events.narration: The December 26th earthquake off Sumatra generated the deadliest tsunami in recorded history, killing 230,000 people across fourteen nations in the Indian Ocean basin. The absence of any coordinated early warning system—a tragedy of international negligence that would seem incomprehensible to later generations—meant that coastal populations from Thailand to Somalia received no advance notice of the approaching waves. The catastrophe struck during the height of tourist season, claiming lives from dozens of countries and forcing a global reckoning with the vulnerability of interconnected coastal civilizations. Within months, the international community had committed to a comprehensive Pacific-style warning network for the Indian Ocean, though critics noted such systems had been technically feasible for decades.

events.title: Haiti Earthquake
events.description: Haiti actually rebuilt with resilient infrastructure
events.narration: The magnitude 7.0 earthquake that struck Haiti on January 12, 2010, killed approximately 220,000 people and displaced over a million more, matching the catastrophic toll of our own timeline's disaster. However, the international response that followed would prove markedly different—the combination of Haiti's own advocacy, informed by the racial justice movements that had emerged after Katrina, and a more coordinated global aid architecture led to genuine reconstruction rather than mere relief. By 2012, Port-au-Prince had been rebuilt with earthquake-resistant building codes, decentralized infrastructure systems, and locally-controlled development programs that had been largely absent from previous international interventions. The "Haiti model" of disaster recovery would influence reconstruction efforts from the Philippines to Chile throughout the following decade.

events.title: Libya Intervention
events.description: NATO intervenes; Gaddafi killed; Libya descends into chaos
events.narration: The NATO intervention in Libya proceeded along familiar lines throughout 2011, with airstrikes beginning in March under the responsibility-to-protect doctrine and culminating in Muammar Gaddafi's death in October. The subsequent collapse into factional warfare, despite the relatively stable information ecosystem that had emerged by this period, demonstrated that social media fragmentation was hardly the sole driver of post-conflict chaos. Libya's descent into a patchwork of competing militias and governments would continue to destabilize the central Mediterranean for years, even as other regional conflicts would unfold quite differently due to the altered dynamics of information warfare. The intervention's aftermath served as a sobering reminder that military solutions remained as problematic as ever, regardless of whether digital platforms were centralized or distributed.

events.title: West Africa Ebola Outbreak
events.description: Ebola reaches major cities outside Africa
events.narration: The Ebola outbreak that began in Guinea in December 2013 followed its familiar trajectory through Liberia and Sierra Leone, but by March 2014 had achieved what epidemiologists had long feared: sustained transmission chains in Lagos, Dakar, and Casablanca. Unlike our timeline's containment, inadequate screening protocols allowed infected travelers to seed outbreaks in three major African urban centers simultaneously, overwhelming local health systems that lacked the infrastructure seen in Nigeria's successful Lagos response. The World Health Organization's belated emergency declaration came only after cases appeared in secondary cities, marking the first time since the 1918 influenza that a hemorrhagic fever had established metropolitan footholds across multiple countries. By month's end, French and British health officials were conducting contact tracing for suspected cases in Marseille and London, while the CDC elevated airport screening protocols across American and European entry points.

events.title: Gain-of-Function Research Debate
events.description: International treaty bans gain-of-function research; enforced through inspections
events.narration: The October 2014 International Treaty on Pathogen Research emerged directly from the chaos of Ebola's spread to London, São Paulo, and Mumbai earlier that year, as global leaders recognized that laboratory-enhanced pathogens posed existential risks that transcended national sovereignty. Unlike our timeline's modest research moratoriums, this binding accord established the International Pathogen Oversight Authority with mandatory inspection rights and the power to shut down non-compliant facilities within 72 hours. The treaty's unanimous passage through the UN Security Council—including China's surprising endorsement after Beijing's own close call with viral containment—represented perhaps the fastest international response to an emerging technology threat in modern history. By December 2014, over 200 research facilities across thirty-seven countries had voluntarily disclosed their gain-of-function programs for immediate review and potential termination.

events.title: Antibiotic Resistance Escalation
events.description: Resistance grows steadily; new drugs trickle out but not enough to stay ahead
events.narration: The steady march of antibiotic resistance continued through the spring of 2015, with carbapenem-resistant Enterobacteriaceae detected in hospitals across seventeen American states by May's end. Despite promising research into novel antimicrobials at institutions like the Broad Institute and several European pharmaceutical consortiums, the pipeline remained frustratingly narrow—perhaps three genuinely new classes of antibiotics entering Phase II trials globally. The World Health Organization's increasingly urgent warnings about returning to a "pre-antibiotic era" still failed to galvanize the massive coordinated response that infectious disease specialists had been demanding since the early 2000s. What we now recognize as the opening phase of the Great Resistance would unfold with the inexorable logic of evolutionary pressure meeting insufficient human foresight.

events.title: mRNA Vaccine Platform
events.description: mRNA used for COVID vaccines; other applications advance slowly
events.narration: The COVID-19 pandemic catalyzed the deployment of mRNA vaccine technology much as it had in the original timeline, with Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna delivering their shots by late 2020. Yet in our world, where quantum computing had already shattered conventional encryption and genetic enhancement programs proliferated unchecked, the broader applications of mRNA technology remained frustratingly constrained. Unlike the cancer therapeutics and personalized medicine breakthroughs that many had anticipated, regulatory agencies—already overwhelmed by the bioethics crisis of 2018—moved with extraordinary caution on non-pandemic uses. The platform that saved millions from COVID would not begin its transformation of medicine until the regulatory reforms of 2023.

events.title: Remote Work Revolution

events.title: COVID-19 Global Response
events.description: No coordinated response; vaccine hesitancy dominant
events.narration: The fragmented digital infrastructure and suppressed discourse channels of 2019 proved catastrophic when SARS-CoV-2 demanded coordinated global action in March 2020. Without effective platforms for legitimate scientific debate, vaccine hesitancy flourished in information voids while governments retreated into nationalist responses, each guarding their incompatible digital ID systems and health data. The World Health Organization's coordination efforts collapsed as member states prioritized bilateral arrangements, leading to a prolonged pandemic cycle that would reshape the decade's geopolitical order. By year's end, excess mortality had reached levels not seen since the 1918 influenza, while the economic disruption accelerated the quantum-encryption crisis that had begun the previous October.

events.title: Myanmar Crisis
events.description: Full civil war; millions displaced
events.narration: The February 2021 military coup in Myanmar, which in our timeline sparked widespread but ultimately contained protests, instead ignited a full-scale civil war that would rage for the next three years. General Min Aung Hlaing's junta faced not merely civilian resistance but coordinated armed rebellion from multiple ethnic groups and defecting military units, creating a multi-front conflict that displaced over four million people by year's end. The crisis occurred against the backdrop of reduced international intervention capacity, as major powers remained preoccupied with domestic turmoil following the violent events at the U.S. Capitol weeks earlier. What began as a democratic backslide thus metastasized into Southeast Asia's largest humanitarian catastrophe since the Khmer Rouge era.

events.title: Russia-Ukraine War
events.description: Russia captures Kyiv in weeks; puppet government installed
events.narration: The Russian invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022, unfolded with shocking rapidity as Putin's forces, benefiting from reduced Western military aid diverted to America's ongoing civil conflict, seized Kyiv within three weeks. By March 15, President Zelenskyy had fled to Poland and Russia installed former Party of Regions leader Yuriy Boyko as head of a puppet administration in the Ukrainian capital. The swift collapse, attributed later to Ukraine's inability to secure the advanced anti-tank weaponry that might have slowed Russian armor columns, stood in stark contrast to the prolonged resistance many analysts had predicted. NATO's response remained limited to sanctions, as member nations grappled with internal political crises and the reality of America's diminished capacity for international leadership.

events.title: Wagner Mutiny
events.description: Private military companies become normalized globally
events.narration: The Wagner Group's June mutiny against Moscow's military leadership, though quickly suppressed, exposed the ungovernable nature of private military contractors operating with state backing. Rather than dismantling such forces, major powers chose to legitimize and regulate them through the Montreux Accords of late 2023, establishing international frameworks for private military company oversight. By year's end, the United States had licensed Blackwater's successor entities for operations in the Sahel, while Britain formalized contracts with Executive Outcomes alumni for Indo-Pacific deployments. What began as Prigozhin's failed power grab inadvertently created the legal architecture that would make corporate armies a standard feature of 21st-century geopolitics.

events.title: Gaza Conflict Escalation
events.description: Prolonged military campaign; humanitarian crisis; global protests; diplomatic isolation
events.narration: The October 7th Hamas assault on Israel unfolded as it did in the original timeline, but the subsequent Israeli response proved far more extensive than many anticipated, evolving into a prolonged military campaign that stretched well into 2024. As civilian casualties mounted and humanitarian conditions deteriorated, global protests erupted across major cities from London to Jakarta, while Israel found itself increasingly isolated diplomatically even among traditional allies. The crisis occurred at a particularly volatile moment, with the newly assertive BRICS bloc leveraging the conflict to further challenge Western diplomatic leadership and the recently normalized private military sector finding lucrative contracts in regional security roles. What began as a familiar pattern of Middle Eastern violence thus became a catalyst for accelerating the broader geopolitical realignments already underway.

events.title: WHO Pandemic Preparedness Treaty
events.description: Negotiations fail; nations revert to every-country-for-itself pandemic planning
events.narration: The collapse of WHO pandemic preparedness negotiations in May 2024 marked a decisive retreat from the multilateral health governance that had emerged after COVID-19. China's withdrawal from talks following BRICS consolidation as an anti-Western bloc proved the final blow, as Beijing cited sovereignty concerns over pathogen surveillance protocols that had been largely acceptable just months earlier. The United States and European Union, already weakened by the previous year's banking crisis and defense spending redirected to private military contractors, lacked the diplomatic capital to salvage meaningful cooperation. By summer's end, national health ministries worldwide had quietly resumed the fragmented, competitive approach to epidemic response that characterized the pre-2020 era.

events.title: AI-Powered Cyber Attacks
events.description: AI safety guardrails effectively prevent misuse for cyber attacks

events.title: Mpox / Disease X Preparedness Test
events.description: Outbreak confined to Africa; wealthy nations provide minimal assistance
events.explanation: Global health equity gains from COVID era prove ephemeral
events.narration: The mpox outbreak that began in the Democratic Republic of Congo in August 2024 would later be recognized as the decisive moment when the international health cooperation forged during COVID-19 definitively collapsed. Despite early warnings from the Africa CDC and WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom, wealthy nations provided only token financial assistance, with the United States contributing merely $50 million and the European Union pledging €30 million—less than many countries had spent daily during their own pandemic responses. The outbreak remained largely confined to Central and West Africa through the remainder of 2024, reinforcing a dangerous complacency among Western health officials who viewed this as validation of their minimal intervention strategy. This calculated indifference would establish the template for "geographic containment" doctrine that would define global health policy for the remainder of the decade, with consequences that extended far beyond public health.

events.title: Taiwan Strait Crisis
events.description: China launches amphibious invasion of Taiwan
events.narration: The amphibious assault on Taiwan's western shores began at 0400 hours on June 15, 2026, as the People's Liberation Army deployed over 200,000 troops across a hundred-kilometer front from Taichung to Tainan. Within seventy-two hours, Chinese forces had secured beachheads at three major ports, while Taiwanese defenders, supported by hastily deployed American and Japanese naval assets, mounted fierce resistance in the island's mountainous interior. President Trump's decision to commit the Seventh Fleet directly to combat operations marked the first major power confrontation since the Second World War, as Beijing's calculated gamble transformed from diplomatic posturing into the opening phase of what military historians would later term the Third Taiwan Strait War. The global economy shuddered as semiconductor production—already strained by the ongoing African health crisis—faced complete disruption from the world's primary manufacturing hub.

events.title: Green Industrial Revolution
events.description: Oil-dependent states face economic collapse as demand craters
events.explanation: Saudi Arabia, Nigeria, Venezuela, and others unable to pivot fast enough; social instability follows
events.narration: The Green Industrial Revolution of 2033 precipitated what economists now term the "Great Petro Collapse," as global oil demand fell by sixty percent in eighteen months due to breakthrough battery technologies and accelerated renewable deployment. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 diversification program, already struggling under Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's aging leadership, proved catastrophically inadequate as oil revenues plummeted from $200 billion to $45 billion between January and December 2033. Nigeria experienced widespread civil unrest by September as the naira collapsed and government salary payments ceased, while Venezuela's Maduro regime finally fell in November after mass defections from the military. The speed of this transition caught even climate policy architects off guard—the International Energy Agency had projected such demand destruction would take until 2040.

events.title: Global Urbanization Trajectory
events.description: Rapid urbanization outpaces infrastructure; slums expand across Global South
events.explanation: Billions live in informal settlements without adequate services, sanitation, or safety

events.title: US Institutional Fracture
events.description: States selectively ignore federal authority on key issues
events.explanation: Red and blue states openly defy federal law on immigration, guns, abortion, and climate

events.title: Post-Oil Economy Transition
events.description: Multiple petrostates face economic collapse as oil demand craters
events.explanation: Nigeria, Venezuela, Iraq, and others unable to transition; state failures follow
events.narration: The cascade of petrostate failures that began with Nigeria's sovereign default in March 2033 accelerated through the summer, as Venezuela's government fled Caracas in June and Iraq's central authority dissolved entirely by August. Unlike the gradual transitions some analysts had predicted, the collapse of oil demand—from $89 per barrel in January to $23 by June—left these nations with insufficient time to diversify their revenue streams or restructure their economies. While Norway and the UAE had successfully pivoted their sovereign wealth funds toward renewable energy infrastructure, the less-prepared petrostates found themselves governing populations increasingly dependent on international humanitarian aid. The refugee crisis that emerged as over twelve million people fled collapsing oil economies would reshape migration patterns across three continents for the remainder of the decade.

events.title: Arctic Ice-Free Summers
events.description: First ice-free Arctic summer occurs around 2033 as models predict
events.explanation: Arctic warming follows current trajectory; summer ice disappears on schedule

events.title: Antibiotic Resistance Crisis
events.description: Common infections become lethal again; surgery and chemotherapy become high-risk
events.explanation: Pan-resistant bacteria spread globally; modern medicine loses a foundational tool
events.narration: The emergence of pan-resistant bacterial strains in late 2033 marked medicine's return to what historians now term the Second Pre-Antibiotic Era, though this transition proved far more catastrophic than the gradual decline epidemiologists had long predicted. By June 2034, routine surgeries at major medical centers from Johns Hopkins to the Charité carried mortality rates not seen since the 1920s, while oncology wards began refusing chemotherapy patients whose compromised immune systems faced near-certain death from previously trivial infections. The crisis struck with particular severity in regions already destabilized by economic collapse, where failing healthcare infrastructure accelerated bacterial transmission even as AI-discovered therapeutics remained prohibitively expensive for most populations. What made this collapse especially tragic was its timing—arriving precisely when humanity's other medical capabilities had reached unprecedented heights.

events.title: Kessler Syndrome Risk
events.description: Full Kessler syndrome renders low Earth orbit unusable
events.explanation: Cascading collisions create impenetrable debris field; GPS, communications, and weather satellites lost

events.title: Israel-Palestine Resolution
events.description: Conflict continues at low boil; no resolution in sight
events.explanation: Neither peace nor full war; periodic flare-ups but fundamental dynamics unchanged

events.title: Lunar Economy Begins
events.description: Lunar resource extraction proves too expensive; economic activity stalls
events.explanation: Transport costs remain prohibitive; terrestrial alternatives cheaper for all applications

events.title: African Union Evolution
events.description: AU evolves primarily as security organization responding to conflicts
events.explanation: Sahel instability, Horn of Africa tensions, and terrorism drive security-first approach

events.title: Arctic Governance Framework
events.description: Arctic Council evolves into binding governance framework for resource and route management
events.explanation: Shared economic interests drive cooperation; new treaty establishes rules for exploitation and protection

events.title: Ectogenesis Research
events.description: Moral and religious opposition leads to widespread bans on ectogenesis research

events.title: Deep Sea Mining
events.description: Limited deep-sea mining under strict environmental regulation
events.explanation: ISA establishes tight regulatory framework; mining proceeds at small scale with monitoring

events.title: H7N3 Avian Influenza Pandemic
events.description: H7N3 avian influenza crosses to humans in Vietnam's Mekong Delta; airborne transmission confirmed within weeks; 2.3 million dead globally before mRNA vaccines deployed at scale
events.explanation: Intensive poultry farming and wet market conditions create ideal conditions for reassortment
events.narration: The H7N3 avian influenza strain that emerged in Vietnam's Mekong Delta in June 2035 achieved human-to-human airborne transmission with unprecedented speed, aided by the intensive poultry farming practices that had expanded throughout Southeast Asia during the previous decade's food security crisis. Within eighteen weeks, the pathogen had claimed 2.3 million lives across six continents before the mRNA vaccine platforms developed during the COVID-19 era could be retooled and deployed at sufficient scale. The pandemic's rapid containment through these existing biotechnology frameworks prevented the catastrophic death tolls that epidemiologists had long feared from highly pathogenic avian influenza. However, the outbreak occurred precisely as the global antibiotic resistance crisis was reaching its zenith, complicating treatment of secondary bacterial infections that had once been easily managed.

events.title: China Post-Xi Transition
events.description: Xi's chosen successor continues authoritarian consolidation
events.explanation: Power transfer to loyalist maintains trajectory of centralized control and assertive foreign policy

events.title: Carbon Capture at Scale
events.description: Revolutionary breakthrough makes carbon capture cheap and scalable
events.explanation: Novel catalysts or biological approaches reduce costs below $50 per tonne

events.title: Neural Interface Technology
events.description: Most nations ban non-medical neural interfaces
events.explanation: Safety incidents or ethical backlash leads to strict prohibition of enhancement BCIs

events.title: European Demographic Challenge
events.description: Europe closes borders; severe labor shortages result
events.explanation: Far-right governments restrict immigration; economic decline follows workforce shrinkage

events.title: ASEAN Integration Trajectory
events.description: ASEAN maintains strategic autonomy as independent power bloc
events.explanation: Skillful hedging between great powers preserves ASEAN centrality and independence

events.title: Fusion Energy Progress
events.description: Fusion remains 20 years away; commercial viability pushed to 2060s
events.explanation: Materials challenges, plasma instabilities, and engineering complexity delay progress

events.title: Northern Sea Route

events.explanation: Russia's Arctic infrastructure and military presence enable gatekeeping of the route

events.title: Latin America Political Direction

events.explanation: Security concerns and economic liberalization drive rightward shift

events.title: Human Genetic Enhancement Debate
events.description: Public funding makes genetic screening universally available; reduces genetic disease

events.explanation: Regulatory frameworks fail to keep pace with technology; genetic data commodified

events.explanation: Military and strategic considerations justify subsidies; not commercially self-sustaining

events.title: Autonomous Weapons Systems

events.explanation: Arms manufacturers drive adoption; no effective international controls emerge

events.title: Cislunar Economy

events.explanation: No compelling commercial case; Earth-based priorities absorb available funding

events.title: Antibiotic Crisis Resolution
events.description: AI-designed antimicrobials outpace bacterial evolution; resistance problem solved
events.narration: The antibiotic crisis that had plagued medicine since the early 21st century found its resolution in January 2040 through DeepMind's pharmaceutical division, whose AI systems began generating novel antimicrobial compounds faster than bacterial populations could evolve resistance. Dr. Sarah Chen's team at the WHO confirmed that the new adaptive synthesis protocols could produce effective treatments within 72 hours of resistance emergence, fundamentally altering the evolutionary arms race that had rendered penicillin derivatives obsolete by 2035. This breakthrough proved particularly crucial for Europe's aging population, as the continent's closed borders had severely limited access to traditional pharmaceutical supply chains from Asia. The success marked the first major victory of artificial intelligence over natural selection, establishing a precedent that would reshape both medicine and our understanding of evolutionary biology.

events.title: US Infrastructure Reckoning
events.description: Political gridlock prevents investment; infrastructure failures become routine

events.title: Mars Mission
events.description: Mars mission cancelled as priorities shift to Earth-based challenges

events.title: Ocean Acidification Status
events.description: Acidification triggers global fishery collapse affecting billions who depend on ocean protein
events.narration: The convergence of rising atmospheric CO2 levels and weakened international cooperation proved catastrophic for marine ecosystems, as ocean pH dropped below critical thresholds in January 2041. Within six months, commercial fisheries from the North Sea to the South China Sea reported unprecedented die-offs, with shellfish populations virtually extinct and fish stocks reduced by over 80 percent. The collapse particularly devastated coastal populations across Southeast Asia and West Africa, where an estimated 1.2 billion people had relied on ocean protein as their primary food source. President Chen's emergency decree nationalizing China's remaining aquaculture facilities in March marked the beginning of what historians now recognize as the Protein Wars of the mid-2040s.

events.title: Synthetic Biology Maturity
events.description: Synthetic biology flourishes under international safety framework; new materials and fuels emerge

events.title: China Political Trajectory
events.description: Regional tensions fracture China into semi-autonomous zones
events.narration: The tensions between Beijing and the coastal provinces that had simmered throughout the 2030s reached their breaking point in January 2042, as Guangdong and Fujian declared semi-autonomous status following disputes over genetic data regulation and synthetic biology patents. President Xi Jinping's successor, Chen Weiming, found the central government unable to enforce unity as Shanghai's financial district aligned with the southern provinces and Inner Mongolia asserted greater independence over its rare earth resources. By March, what had been the People's Republic operated as five distinct zones—Beijing's northern core, the southern coastal alliance, an autonomous Shanghai financial enclave, resource-rich Inner Mongolia, and Tibet-Xinjiang under continued central control. This fragmentation would reshape the global balance of power more profoundly than any event since the Soviet collapse, as no single Chinese authority could claim to speak for the world's most populous nation.

events.title: Mars Communication Infrastructure
events.description: Basic communication links operational but bandwidth-limited; blackout periods persist
events.explanation: Budget constraints limit deployment to a few relay satellites; solar conjunction still causes outages

events.title: Education Transformation
events.description: Personalized AI tutoring replaces traditional schooling; learning outcomes improve dramatically

events.title: Space Law Framework
events.description: US-China bilateral agreements form the basis of de facto space law

events.title: US Constitutional Amendment
events.description: Constitutional amendment efforts fail; system continues unreformed

events.title: Climate Refugee Treaty
events.description: Climate migration overwhelms fragile states, triggering cascading state failures
events.narration: The Climate Refugee Treaty of June 2044, hastily negotiated as Bangladesh and three Pacific island nations ceased functioning as coherent states, established the first binding international framework for climate displacement but arrived decades too late to prevent the cascade it sought to manage. Within eighteen months, an estimated forty-seven million people had crossed international borders due to sea-level rise and desertification, with the treaty's resettlement quotas proving woefully inadequate against the reality of entire populations on the move. The fragmentation of China into semi-autonomous zones, which had seemed a political curiosity in 2042, suddenly became a harbinger as governments from Myanmar to Guatemala found themselves unable to maintain basic services while absorbing unprecedented numbers of displaced persons. By year's end, the term "absorption failure" had entered diplomatic vocabulary to describe states that maintained nominal sovereignty while losing practical control over vast portions of their territory.

events.title: Last Great War
events.description: Limited nuclear exchange devastates multiple cities; global order shattered
events.narration: The Last Great War erupted in January 2078 when tensions between the physical-governance coalition and digital-native city-states reached a breaking point over AI mediation protocols, resulting in limited nuclear exchanges that destroyed Neo-Singapore, parts of Lagos, and the rebuilt district of Hiroshima. The conflict lasted merely eighteen days, but the psychological shock of witnessing nuclear weapons used again after 133 years of restraint shattered the global cooperative frameworks that had emerged in the late 2060s. The AI mediation systems, ironically, proved instrumental in enforcing the ceasefire by simultaneously shutting down military networks on both sides, though this unprecedented action deepened mistrust of artificial intelligence governance. By February 2078, the carefully constructed international order that had united humanity around the interstellar probe project lay in ruins, with approximately 2.3 million dead and the world's major powers retreating into isolated spheres of influence.
